title: Race-lap query ranking instead of plain binary classification
summary: Pit calls are relative within a race/lap context: teams choose who among active cars is most pit-ready now.
rationale: Pit calls are relative within a race/lap context: teams choose who among active cars is most pit-ready now. CatBoost ranking objectives support grouped optimization and preserve high-cardinality categoricals with ordered CTRs, which fits `Driver`, `Race`, and compound interactions better than manual encodings.
implementation_hint: Create groups at `Race_Year x LapNumber` or `Race_Year x LapNumber x compound_regime`; train a ranker such as `YetiRankPairwise` or `QueryCrossEntropy` on `PitNextLap` labels, then map groupwise scores back to probabilities via OOF logistic calibration or monotone scaling. Keep current strong handcrafted numeric features, but pass raw categoricals natively.
expected_effect: Better AUC by learning pit priority within each lap and reducing loss from globally miscalibrated thresholds.
risk: Some groups may have few positives or none; probability recovery from ranking scores can be noisy if calibration is not fully OOF.
